title: Instrumentality and pregnancy
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality and pregnancy             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sun Jul 19, 2015 4:32 pm If a woman who is pregnant got tanged from 3I and returned from the sea of LCL, would she still be pregnant, or would the child be lost forever?

[]: Re: Here's a bizarre question             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jul 19, 2015 4:35 pm Arcadia's legacy wrote: would she still be pregnant?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 19, 2015 5:22 pm Hmm... depends if it has a soul of it's own or not yet, doesn't it? Because the fetus might have to imagine itself back into life on it's own and if so there's no guarantee both will come back together.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Sun Jul 19, 2015 6:38 pm Ah, but if the woman imagines herself as pregnant, what then?

[]:             Quote Post by JonnyNova » Sun Jul 19, 2015 7:03 pm BlueBasilisk wrote: Ah, but if the woman imagines herself as pregnant, what then?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Jul 19, 2015 8:02 pm My opinion is that yes, she would. While it's growing, the fetus is a symbiote/parasite attached to the mother, and effectively part of her. Therefore I say yes, it would come back with her.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Jul 19, 2015 10:45 pm In my fanfic, the children are lost forever, come back weird and look nothing like their parents so no one know who belongs to who. There are also a bunch of fetuses that wash ashore, and simply die. I mean I wanted to go the happy route and have Lillith or/and Adam lead/put the children back into their mother's womb but I felt a darker route would be more realistic. I had creepy Cronenbergian ideas too.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Jul 22, 2015 6:02 am This is basically the old question of when human life starts. Difficult to answer as it depends on society/individual to answer for oneself.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 22, 2015 4:25 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: My opinion is that yes, she would. While it's growing, the fetus is a symbiote/parasite attached to the mother, and effectively part of her. Therefore I say yes, it would come back with her.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Wed Jul 22, 2015 5:57 pm Then a child who already "budded off" is at risk of not coming back. I mean, how could they imagine themselves as individuals if they haven't even developed a concept of individuality yet? It's only some time after birth that babies start examining their hands, touching their own bodies, etc.; way I see it, it's only at that time that the child fully actualizes as an independent entity separate from the environment. Even then, I don't think children would have any reason to come back to reality, since they can have anything they want and most likely wouldn't be able to recognize the Instrumentality-created illusions of their parents as not the real ones.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 22, 2015 6:32 pm amitakartok wrote: Then a child who already "budded off" is at risk of not coming back. I mean, how could they imagine themselves as individuals if they haven't even developed a concept of individuality yet?

[]:             Quote Post by zlink64 » Wed Jul 22, 2015 8:25 pm Maybe unborn babies imagine themselves as part of their mother. Like the womb is them or part of how they see themselves so they unintentionally follow their mothers and pregnant mothers imagine there unborn babies as part of themselves so it all just kinda works out if the mother begins to return. I remember reading something that said that part of the reason why Asuka was with Shinji at the end was because he wished for/desired it? If I'm remembering that correctly maybe mothers who return can wish for their already born babies to be with them. We must save the babies lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 22, 2015 8:29 pm zlink64 wrote: I remember reading something that said that part of the reason why Asuka was with Shinji at the end was because he wished for it?

[]:             Quote Post by zlink64 » Wed Jul 22, 2015 8:32 pm I dunno. Just something I remember reading somewhere here in old threads but if you don't know it chances are whatever I read wasn't like official.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 22, 2015 8:36 pm zlink64 wrote: I dunno. Just something I remember reading somewhere here on these threads but if you don't know it chances are whatever I read wasn't like official.

[]:             Quote Post by zlink64 » Wed Jul 22, 2015 9:17 pm Kk, I found what I was remembering. Apparently it's from one of the cards in the card game : "In the sea of LCL, Shinji wished for a world with other people. He desired to meet them again, even if it meant he would be hurt and betrayed. And just as he had hoped/wanted, Asuka was present in the new world. Only Asuka was there beside him. The girl who he had hurt, and by whom he had been hurt. But even so, she was the one he had hoped/wished for...." Now that I'm rereading it though it doesn't really imply what I was saying in the first place ,so, hypothetically even if this was true it wouldn't matter in relation to the babies. The toddlers are done for I think. Even if they came back there be like an army of orphans crawling from the ocean with no mommies.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jul 22, 2015 9:56 pm The flowery, metaphorical way the process of returning is described might just be a way of describing life's general survival instinct. In that case, everyone and everything probably comes back -- at least unless it has a human mind sufficiently devastated to counteract that default. That's a lot easier to work with and answers these kinds of questions, including why trees come back. Even a broken wooden telephone pole comes back -- complete with metal and wires attached.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 22, 2015 10:15 pm If babies don't get their own souls from their mothers until they're born/leave the mother's body I would think the whole issue would go away.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jul 22, 2015 11:00 pm Maybe the parallels to the pilot and the Eva and the baby in the womb could shed some light on this discussion. (The power cable is called an "umbilical chord," Asuka even playfully mentions an entry plug as a "womb," ect.) If we can decide what the show thinks of a pilot's soul while inside the Eva, it might give us clues as to how a baby's soul might operate while in the mother's womb.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 22, 2015 11:59 pm Monk Ed wrote: Even a broken wooden telephone pole comes back -- complete with metal and wires attached.
